extraction.one_line_summary: MOHORIČ held off DENZ from the breakaway after DUMOULIN suffered late bike change but limited losses to 18 seconds.
tactical_patterns: CICCONE harvested both KOM climbs from early break, signaling selective aggression for jersey competitions over stage victory.
Mitchelton-Scott abandoned CHAVES chase around 2 minutes back, preserving domestiques rather than burning team defending pink jersey.
Quick-Step delayed sprint train setup while monitoring VIVIANI's position, revealing nervousness about controlling fragmented finales.
DUMOULIN bike change at 18km triggered no panic pursuit, suggesting Sky calculated pink jersey gap safe despite isolation.

rider_signals.MOHORIČ Matej: positive: won two-up sprint after cooperating with DENZ through finale, showing tactical maturity to share work then outsprint
rider_signals.DENZ Nico: positive: forced MOHORIČ to cooperate until final meters despite previous failed break attempts same race
rider_signals.CHAVES Esteban: negative: distanced when Sky accelerated on rolling roads, lost 10+ minutes suggesting form collapse or illness mid-stage
rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: neutral: bike change 18km out cost time but no teammates rallied suggesting calculated acceptance of small GC loss
rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: contested intermediate sprint in pink jersey despite no GC threat, extending Dumoulin gap to 41 seconds aggressively
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: sprinted from chase group for third despite breakaway finishing clear, maximizing points when stage win unavailable
rider_signals.CICCONE Giulio: positive: prioritized both early KOMs over break cohesion, tactical focus on jersey classification rather than stage result
